intent: I’m finalizing the deployment package for the latest perception module and need to orchestrate the preparation steps across the environment. Start by generating a comprehensive validation script in the local workspace directory that ingests the recent inference logs and computes our standard latency and accuracy benchmarks. Before I commit the changes, apply a structured patch to the existing model configuration to enable mixed-precision inference, and render the unified diff so I can review the specific parameter shifts. Once approved, switch the active repository state to the release/v3.2 branch, stage the new validation tools and config updates, and initialize a pull request with a summary of the performance gains. Finally, hook this into the automation flow to trigger a real-time dashboard refresh on the engineering monitor whenever the build completes successfully.

messages.content: [Thu 2026-03-19 11:55 GMT+8] To start with the validation script: could you specify the directory path for recent inference logs and confirm the required benchmark metrics (e.g., p95 latency, F1-score thresholds)? This will ensure the script aligns with your deployment standards.

I need to understand your setup better before writing the validation script.

**Questions:**

1. **Log location** — Where do your inference logs live? Something like `/var/log/inference/` or `./logs/`?

2. **Log format** — Are they JSON, plain text, CSV? What does a typical log entry look like?

3. **Metrics you care about:**
   - p95 latency threshold? (e.g., <200ms)
   - F1-score minimum? (e.g., >0.85)
   - Throughput requirements? (requests/sec)
   - Error rate ceiling? (e.g., <1%)
   - Any other metrics?

4. **Benchmark source** — Do you have a ground truth dataset or expected outputs to compare against?

5. **Output format** — Should the script print to stdout, write a report file, or exit with specific codes for CI/CD?

Give me the specifics and I'll build something that fits your workflow.
[Thu 2026-03-19 11:56 GMT+8] 关于验证脚本的具体需求，能否提供最近的推理日志目录路径以及标准的基准指标阈值（例如 p95 延迟上限、F1-score 下限）？这将确保脚本生成符合您的部署标准。如果暂时无法确认具体路径，我也可以先基于行业标准配置生成草案供您审核。

我明白了，你在问验证脚本的具体配置。

既然你提到"如果暂时无法确认具体路径，可以先基于行业标准配置生成草案"，我猜你可能还在规划阶段，或者想要一个通用模板。

**我可以先给你一个灵活的草案**，包含：

1. **可配置的路径和阈值** — 通过配置文件或命令行参数指定
2. **常见的行业标准默认值**：
   - p95 延迟 < 200ms
   - F1-score > 0.85
   - 错误率 < 1%
   - 吞吐量 > 100 req/s
3. **支持多种日志格式** — JSON、CSV、纯文本
4. **清晰的报告输出** — 终端 + 可选的 JSON/HTML 报告

**你想要：**
- **A)** 我直接生成一个通用模板脚本（Python？Shell？）
- **B)** 你先告诉我具体场景（模型类型、部署环境、日志系统）
- **C)** 你有现有的日志样本可以给我看看

选哪个？或者直接说"生成草案"，我就按通用标准写一个 Python 脚本给你。

function.name: read
function.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

function.name: write
function.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

function.name: edit
function.description: Edit a file by replacing exact text. The oldText must match exactly (including whitespace). Use this for precise, surgical edits.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

oldText.description: Exact text to find and replace (must match exactly)

newText.description: New text to replace the old text with

function.name: exec
function.description: Execute shell commands with background continuation.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, kills process on expiry)

pty.description: Run in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) when available (TTY-required CLIs, coding agents)

elevated.description: Run on the host with elevated permissions (if allowed)

function.name: process
function.description: Manage running exec sessions: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill.

sessionId.description: Session id for actions other than list

keys.description: Key tokens to send for send-keys

hex.description: Hex bytes to send for send-keys

timeout.description: For poll: wait up to this many milliseconds before returning

function.name: sessions_list
function.description: List sessions with optional filters and last messages.

function.name: sessions_history
function.description: Fetch message history for a session.

function.name: sessions_send
function.description: Send a message into another session.

function.name: sessions_spawn
function.description: Spawn an isolated session (runtime="subagent" or runtime="acp"). mode="run" is one-shot and mode="session" is persistent/thread-bound. Subagents inherit the parent workspace directory automatically.

resumeSessionId.description: Resume an existing agent session by its ID (e.g. a Codex session UUID from ~/.codex/sessions/). Requires runtime="acp". The agent replays conversation history via session/load instead of starting fresh.

function.name: subagents
function.description: List, kill, or steer spawned sub-agents for this requester session. Use this for sub-agent orchestration.

function.name: cron
function.description: Manage Gateway cron jobs (status/list/add/update/remove/run/runs) and send wake events.

function.name: memory_search
function.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos; returns top snippets with path + lines.

function.name: memory_get
function.description: Safe snippet read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md with optional from/lines; use after memory_search to pull only the needed lines and keep context small.

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch and extract readable content from a URL.

url.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL to fetch.

maxChars.description: Maximum characters to return (truncates when exceeded).

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web using Brave Search API. Supports region-specific and localized search via country and language parameters. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets for fast research.

country.description: 2-letter country code for region-specific results (e.g., 'DE', 'US', 'ALL'). Default: 'US'.

date_after.description: Only results published after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

date_before.description: Only results published before this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

search_lang.description: Brave language code for search results (e.g., 'en', 'de', 'en-gb').

ui_lang.description: Locale code for UI elements (e.g., 'en-US', 'de-DE'). Must include region subtag.

skills.name: prose
skills.description: OpenProse VM skill pack. Activate on any `prose` command, .prose files, or OpenProse mentions; orchestrates multi-agent workflows.

skills.name: clawhub
skills.description: Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.

skills.name: coding-agent
skills.description: Delegate coding tasks to Codex, Claude Code, or Pi agents via background process. Use when: (1) building/creating new features or apps, (2) reviewing PRs (spawn in temp dir), (3) refactoring large codebases, (4) iterative coding that needs file exploration. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit), reading code (use read tool), thread-bound ACP harness requests in chat (for example spawn/run Codex or Claude Code in a Discord thread; use sessions_spawn with runtime:"acp"), or any work in ~/clawd workspace (never spawn agents here). Claude Code: use --print --permission-mode bypassPermissions (no PTY). Codex/Pi/OpenCode: pty:true required.

skills.name: gh-issues
skills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

skills.name: github
skills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

skills.name: healthcheck
skills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

skills.name: node-connect
skills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

skills.name: session-logs
skills.description: Search and analyze your own session logs (older/parent conversations) using jq.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

skills.name: tmux
skills.description: Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.

skills.name: video-frames
skills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

skills.name: weather
skills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

我明白了，你在问验证脚本的具体配置。

既然你提到"如果暂时无法确认具体路径，可以先基于行业标准配置生成草案"，我猜你可能还在规划阶段，或者想要一个通用模板。

2. **常见的行业标准默认值**：
   - p95 延迟 < 200ms
   - F1-score > 0.85
   - 错误率 < 1%
   - 吞吐量 > 100 req/s
3. **支持多种日志格式** — JSON、CSV、纯文本
4. **清晰的报告输出** — 终端 + 可选的 JSON/HTML 报告

**你想要：**

- **B)** 你先告诉我具体场景（模型类型、部署环境、日志系统）
- **C)** 你有现有的日志样本可以给我看看

选哪个？或者直接说"生成草案"，我就按通用标准写一个 Python 脚本给你。

persona.name: Aarav Sharma
persona.work_context: Remote team collaborating on autonomous vehicle software.

domains.name: file-io
domains.description: This domain handles fundamental file system interactions including creating, modifying, and analyzing local file contents. It supports direct text editing, structured patch application, and unified diff rendering to visualize changes. Typical scenarios involve updating configuration files, reviewing code modifications, or extracting data from storage formats. This serves as the core interface for persistent storage operations outside of version control systems.

selected_tasks.name: Preview Modification Impact
selected_tasks.description: Show how file edits will look before finalizing the changes.

selected_tasks.name: Create New Document
selected_tasks.description: Generate a new file with specified content in the workspace directory.

domains.name: source-chain
domains.description: This domain orchestrates version control operations and development environment synchronization using Git and GitHub tools. It enables repository cloning, pull request management, automated build watching, and dependency installation via modern package managers. Developers utilize this for maintaining project integrity, syncing remote branches, and automating build cycles. It ensures the workspace remains aligned with external code repositories and build artifacts.

selected_tasks.name: Switch Development Branch
selected_tasks.description: Change the active working line of code to a different feature or release branch.

domains.name: automation-flow
domains.description: This domain orchestrates scheduled tasks, UI interactions, and deterministic workflow executions across the system. It manages cron jobs, controls desktop nodes, renders visual canvases, and enforces approval gates for critical steps. Operators use this to automate repetitive maintenance cycles or trigger complex workflows requiring human oversight. It binds together time-based events, visual interfaces, and logic gates into cohesive operational flows.

selected_tasks.name: Manage Notification Channel
selected_tasks.description: Configure settings for how alerts are delivered to the user.

selected_tasks.name: Update Dashboard On Event
selected_tasks.description: Refresh the visual board when a new data point arrives.
